name: ETH Inventory Reset Reclaim v1
thesis: ETH 4h is the best-fit similar-asset lane for testing inventory-reset continuation after a flush-and-reclaim rather than static oversold bounce logic. The edge should come from reclaiming value after overextension and then continuing enough to reward staged management.
invalidation_condition: Abort if reclaim setups remain too rare, if direction confirmation conflicts with the recovery logic, or if later exit slices fail to pay for the idea after costs.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A sharp flush resets inventory, price reclaims value, and continuation follows because the panic move was over-extended.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: ETH often expresses reclaim-after-flush structure more cleanly than DOGE while still being close enough in character to validate the concept in the same basket.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: ETH 4h is the cleaner lane for a reclaim continuation mechanism because the recovery structure is usually more orderly and interpretable than on DOGE.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If the market stays balance-dominated instead of transitioning, reclaim moves may bounce and stall before later targets.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries populate but continuation is shallow, reduce runner ambition before tightening the recovery trigger.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; reclaim and directional confirmation align after the reset
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by broad reset thresholds instead of rare extreme events
